topic: Joint identification and matrix-completion estimator for staggered-treatment panels with low-rank unit-time heterogeneity and an explicit recovery-threshold phase transition expressed in observed eigen-gap and treatment-design constants

open_problems.open_problem: Give a necessary-and-sufficient observed spectral/eigen-gap threshold for when low-rank matrix completion, SDiD, ASCM, and staggered-SDiD identify the same ATT and have the same first-order law in staggered panels.

source_refs.claim: Proposes nuclear-norm matrix completion for causal panel counterfactuals and distinguishes regularization-based identification from hard-restriction approaches.
source_refs.author: Athey, Bayati, Doudchenko, Imbens, Khosravi
source_refs.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association

source_refs.claim: Quantifies sample-size and information-theoretic limits for exact low-rank matrix recovery under random observation designs.
source_refs.author: Candes, Tao

source_refs.claim: Frames low-rank matrix completion through spectral recovery and phase-transition behavior for randomly observed entries.
source_refs.author: Keshavan, Montanari, Oh

open_problems.why_unsolved: Published causal-panel matrix completion supplies estimators and asymptotic guarantees for treated counterfactual cells, while classical matrix-completion phase transitions are mostly for random observation masks. The prior CausalSmith angle reached the same obstruction from the estimator-equivalence side: it could not make the pulled-back kernel, active nuclear-norm face, selected subgradient, and rank-R completion class jointly well-posed as an observable finite-dimensional certificate.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A sharp identification theorem with counterexamples: recovery and estimator equality hold iff a deterministic staggered-treatment mask satisfies an observable rank/eigen-gap and cohort-overlap certificate.

open_problems.open_problem: Characterize when equality between ASCM, SDiD, and rank-R matrix-completion functionals is stable across ridge, SDiD quadratic, and nuclear-norm tuning paths in weak-factor or small-eigen-gap regimes.

source_refs.claim: Studies data-driven model selection for causal-panel matrix completion and links selection to permutation inference under assignment mechanisms.
source_refs.author: Heiniger

source_refs.claim: Uses tall/wide factor-imputation blocks for counterfactuals under strong factor structure, with rates and normal theory.
source_refs.author: Bai, Ng
source_refs.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association

source_refs.claim: Shows panel regression inference with interactive fixed effects can avoid consistent factor-number estimation in some regimes.
source_refs.author: Moon, Weidner

source_refs.excerpt: Penalty-stable equality frontier; NOT YET PROVED

open_problems.why_unsolved: Rank choice, ridge penalties, SDiD quadratic penalties, and nuclear-norm shrinkage all change the estimand when the treated block removes entries needed to separate weak factors from noise. The prior proposal flags incompatible published penalty normalizations and only conjectures a positive-codimension exceptional equality set, while recent model-selection work does not give a finite-sample eigen-gap threshold for when tuning is identification-neutral.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A finite-dimensional exceptional-set formula or adaptive sufficient-condition theorem giving tuning/rank rules as functions of observed singular gaps, mask leverage, and treatment-design constants, with counterexamples outside the stated regime.

open_problems.open_problem: Prove or refute the strict SCM/ASCM/SDiD/matrix-completion exact-identification lattice, with adjacent Schur or Riesz certificates for ATT equality and separation.

source_refs.excerpt: Strict span lattice; NOT YET PROVED
determinant-ratio formulas and the minimality ... may need adjustment

open_problems.why_unsolved: The banked proposal withdrew a single spectral-threshold kernel and left the strict inclusion lattice unproved because minimal witness enumeration and determinant-ratio formulas may need adjustment. The obstacle is not merely proving known estimator consistency; it is determining whether each adjacent estimator class has a finite-dimensional certificate that separates its ATT functional from the next class under bounded-rank panel laws.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A finite-dimensional certificate theorem plus minimal bounded-rank panel witnesses for each strict inclusion or a counterexample collapsing two adjacent lattice nodes.

open_problems.open_problem: Unify synthetic DiD weighting and low-rank matrix completion into an estimator that is efficient under staggered adoption and robust to misspecification of either balancing restrictions or low-rank factor structure.

source_refs.claim: Introduces synthetic DiD, combining DiD and synthetic-control ideas, and proves consistency and asymptotic normality under latent factor structure.
source_refs.author: Arkhangelsky, Athey, Hirshberg, Imbens, Wager

source_refs.claim: Generalizes synthetic controls to staggered adoption and shows average-effect error depends on both individual and pooled treated-unit imbalance.
source_refs.author: Ben-Michael, Feller, Rothstein
source_refs.venue: Journal of the Royal Statistical Society Series B

source_refs.claim: Derives efficient estimators for staggered rollout designs where treatment timing is as-good-as randomly assigned.
source_refs.author: Roth, Sant'Anna

open_problems.why_unsolved: SDiD weights target robustness and efficiency through balancing, whereas matrix completion regularizes a latent untreated-outcome surface. Existing results do not provide a joint semiparametric efficiency bound or orthogonal score for staggered low-rank panels that remains valid when either the balancing restrictions or the low-rank recovery conditions are misspecified.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: An orthogonal-score estimator with balancing weights and low-rank nuisance estimates, plus an efficiency bound and a double-robust consistency theorem for staggered low-rank panels.

open_problems.open_problem: Construct honest finite-sample and asymptotic inference for dynamic treatment effects when untreated counterfactuals are estimated by low-rank matrix completion under staggered adoption.

source_refs.claim: Gives SVD-based entrywise inference for missing panel data induced by staggered adoption, with non-asymptotic bounds and instance-optimal confidence intervals.
source_refs.author: Yan, Wainwright

source_refs.claim: Develops inference for low-rank completion without sample splitting and applies it to average treatment effect estimation.
source_refs.author: Choi, Kwon, Liao

source_refs.claim: Provides non-asymptotic prediction intervals for synthetic-control predictands under staggered adoption and uniform post-treatment inference.
source_refs.author: Cattaneo, Feng, Palomba, Titiunik

open_problems.why_unsolved: Entrywise low-rank inference and synthetic-control prediction intervals are advancing, but dynamic event-study inference must account simultaneously for estimated rank, shrinkage bias, correlated panel shocks, cohort aggregation, and many post-treatment contrasts. The missing bridge is a theorem whose coverage constants are expressed in the same observable eigen-gap and design quantities used for recovery.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: Uniform confidence bands for cohort-time and event-time effects after low-rank imputation, with debiasing terms and coverage guarantees depending on observed eigen-gap and treatment-design constants.

open_problems.open_problem: Identify when a low-rank treatment-effect surface can be separated from low-rank untreated interactive fixed effects rather than being absorbed into the matrix-completion counterfactual.

source_refs.claim: Shows TWFE event-study coefficients can be contaminated by treatment effects from other periods under heterogeneous dynamic effects.
source_refs.author: Sun, Abraham

source_refs.claim: Shows TWFE estimators with heterogeneous treatment effects can identify non-convex weighted averages that differ from target effects.
source_refs.author: de Chaisemartin, D'Haultfoeuille

source_refs.claim: Studies treatment-effect learning in panels with general intervention patterns and a target average treatment effect over treated entries.
source_refs.author: Farias, Li, Peng

open_problems.why_unsolved: Low-rank untreated outcomes and low-rank treatment-effect surfaces are observationally entangled because each unit-time cell reveals only one potential outcome. Staggered adoption adds collinearity between event-time effects and missing blocks, so existing heterogeneous-DID warnings do not themselves characterize when two low-rank matrices are jointly recoverable.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A separability or nonidentification theorem characterizing when the untreated outcome matrix and treatment-effect matrix are jointly recoverable from treated/control entries under staggered observation masks.

open_problems.open_problem: Characterize matrix-completion identification when there are no never-treated units, only later-treated and always-treated units.

source_refs.claim: Proposes retrospective counterfactual imputation by matrix completion for panels with later-treated and always-treated units but no never-treated units.
source_refs.author: Poulos, Albanese, Mercatanti, Li

source_refs.claim: Shows staggered-adoption synthetic-control error depends on both individual treated-unit imbalance and pooled imbalance.
source_refs.author: Ben-Michael, Feller, Rothstein
source_refs.venue: Journal of the Royal Statistical Society Series B

open_problems.why_unsolved: Without never-treated donors, untreated potential outcomes must be extrapolated from pre-adoption blocks and later-treated cohorts. Existing proposals supply weighting or imputation workflows, but not a sharp low-rank recovery boundary explaining when the staggered mask retains enough submatrix information for exact counterfactual recovery.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A sharp bound or impossibility theorem for counterfactual recovery using only later-treated blocks, with sufficient conditions involving cohort timing support and low-rank submatrix eigen-gaps.

literature_map:
Athey, Bayati, Doudchenko, Imbens, and Khosravi (2021, JASA) anchor nuclear-norm causal-panel matrix completion for treated counterfactual cells.
Bai and Ng (2021, JASA) and Bai (2009, Econometrica) anchor factor-imputation and interactive fixed-effect counterfactual theory.
Arkhangelsky, Athey, Hirshberg, Imbens, and Wager (2021, AER) anchor SDiD as a balancing-plus-factor estimator.
Ben-Michael, Feller, and Rothstein (2022, JRSSB) extend synthetic controls to staggered adoption and emphasize pooled imbalance.
Callaway and Sant'Anna (2021, Journal of Econometrics), Sun and Abraham (2021, Journal of Econometrics), de Chaisemartin and D'Haultfoeuille (2020, AER), and Borusyak, Jaravel, and Spiess (2024, ReStud) anchor modern staggered-DID/event-study identification and heterogeneity failures.
Roth and Sant'Anna (2021/2023, arXiv) contribute efficient staggered-rollout estimation under design-style timing assumptions.
Choi, Kwon, and Liao (2024, Journal of Econometrics) and Yan and Wainwright (2024, arXiv) push low-rank completion inference, including staggered-adoption missing panels.
Choi and Yuan (2024, JASA) and Agarwal, Dahleh, Shah, and Shen (2023, COLT/PMLR) connect causal matrix completion to MNAR or latent-confounded missingness.
Candes and Tao (2010, IEEE TIT), Keshavan, Montanari, and Oh (2010, IEEE TIT), and Candes-Recht-style results provide classical recovery-threshold anchors, mostly under random masks.
The main tension is that econometric panel papers give estimators and asymptotics for causal masks, while statistical matrix-completion papers give phase transitions mostly outside deterministic staggered-treatment designs.
The live gap for this qid is a theorem-level bridge: observed eigen-gap and treatment-design constants should determine exact recovery, partial identification, or nonrecovery and should feed directly into tuning and inference.
prior_proposal_map:
No substantive current-qid proposal or review file existed for panel_mc_estimation_duality_v1; the current directory held only state and pipeline metadata.
Track A mined doc/formalization/_bank/failed/panel_sdid_mc_id_v1, including the rejected angle0 proposal, the final proposal, and angle0_v5 reviewer JSON.
The strongest prior-proposal signal was panel_sdid_mc_id_v1: spectral equality frontier, strict estimator span lattice, and penalty-stable equality frontier were explicitly marked not yet proved.
Reviewer strengthening_paths for panel_sdid_mc_id_v1 asked for an explicit finite-dimensional observable computation, which cross-linked directly to the web literature gap on deterministic staggered-mask recovery thresholds.
Adjacent panel/DiD bank entries were inspected for fallback gaps: panel_honest_did_phase_v1, pid_did_anticipation_bounded_v1, q1_spectral_phase_transition_p1_markov, pid_synth_control_factor_misspec_v1, and q_did_drdid_ipwdid_frontier_v1.
Those adjacent entries supplied useful but less central panel/partial-ID problems; only the strict SCM/ASCM/SDiD/matrix-completion lattice survived filtering as a standalone prior-proposal entry.
The appended study-pipeline LATE and Manski interval-regression gaps were treated as candidates but not emitted because they do not engage the panel matrix-completion topic anchor.
